content: actually no. I switched to Helix last month. update that
Correcting — your editor is now Helix (switched from NeoVim last month). Updated. The old NeoVim memory will be marked superseded.
and while we're at it, my OS is also wrong. you have me on macOS but I switched to Asahi Linux on my M1 in march
Another correction — you're now on Asahi Linux (M1 hardware), switched in March. macOS memory marked superseded. Anything else stale I should know about?
I think that's it for now. but the brain page should show 'corrected' or something on these. otherwise old memories pollute future agent decisions

Strong point. The bi-temporal substrate (valid_to) we shipped in V1.5 marks superseded memories — they no longer surface in retrieval. V1.6 adds the memory_correct tool that explicitly retires old facts when correcting. The brain page UI surface for 'corrected' is V1.7 work — daily-diff view shows what changed.
ok good. so I'm covered on the backend but not yet on the visible UI
Exactly. Backend won't surface stale facts; UI can't yet show the correction history. V1.7 fills that gap.
